mnemonics: Heuristic Helps Hustle (HHH) - Heuristic Functions help logical agents hustle (search more efficiently) towards a goal.
keyPoints: A heuristic function, h(n), estimates the cost from a node to the goal.
It must be admissible (never overestimates the true cost) to guarantee an optimal solution with algorithms like A*.
It is a core component that guides informed search strategies, making them more efficient than uninformed searches.
commonMistakes: Confusing the estimated cost h(n) with the actual cost g(n) from the start node to the current node.
Assuming a more complex heuristic is always better, when sometimes a simpler one is computationally cheaper and effective enough.
Using a non-admissible heuristic with A* and expecting an optimal solution.
